mapping.a_element: primordial density fluctuation field with a specific statistical distribution (Gaussian or chi^2_1)
mapping.b_element: spatial dimensions of minimal surfaces as solutions to the Plateau problem

mapping.a_element: cluster abundance and S-Z power spectra as observables derived from the density field
mapping.b_element: classical solutions of d-brane configurations derived from minimal surfaces

mapping.a_element: self-consistent calculations of redshift distributions and correlation functions to test model viability
mapping.b_element: derivation of d-brane solutions from geometric constraints (vanishing extrinsic mean curvature)

brief_justification: Paper A relies on statistical field theory and cosmological perturbation calculations to predict cluster abundances and power spectra, while Paper B uses geometric and variational principles (minimal surfaces, Plateau problem) to derive classical solutions for d-branes. The underlying machinery—statistical field distributions vs. geometric minimal surfaces—differs fundamentally, despite both involving solutions derived from constraints.
